---
title: "Mentoring & Advising"
listing:
  id: listing-projects
  contents: "projects/mentoring/*.qmd"
  type: grid
  sort: "date desc"
  categories: true
---

My mentoring and advising work focuses on helping students move from coursework into applied practice, research communication, professional development, and career planning.

Rather than treating mentoring as a single type of interaction, I approach it as a progression: helping students build skills, apply those skills in real projects, communicate their work, and connect their experiences to future opportunities.

## Mentoring Areas

My mentoring work generally falls into three overlapping areas:

- **Applied student project mentoring:** supporting students as they work on community GIS, UAS, environmental data, dashboards, StoryMaps, field data collection, and partner-facing deliverables.
- **Undergraduate research and presentation mentoring:** helping students turn class projects, independent work, and applied projects into posters, presentations, conference submissions, and research communication products.
- **Career coaching and professional advising:** helping students and alumni connect coursework, technical skills, capstone projects, internships, portfolios, and professional goals.

## Approach

I try to help students see themselves as emerging professionals, not just students completing assignments. This means helping them recognize the value of their work, explain their skills clearly, and connect classroom experiences to real opportunities.

Mentoring often involves translating between academic requirements, professional expectations, and individual student goals. The goal is to help students make informed choices, build confidence through experience, and develop the technical and professional skills they need for meaningful next steps.

## Selected Mentoring and Advising Examples

The examples below highlight different ways I support students as they build applied skills, present their work, and prepare for professional pathways.

::: {#listing-projects}
:::
